description: Paired C0-C4 20-percent-off coupon experiment at the 12-vehicle formal nine-zone candidate baseline, including an AgentSociety PublicGoodsAgent allocation arm.

public_goods_game.interpretation: Virtual contribution tokens and multiplied returns create linked cooperation decisions; they do not create physical coupons. Awards combine accessibility need and cooperation score.

coupon_experiment.interpretation: Finite daily 20-percent-off coupon scenario assumptions, not observed Shanghai take-up or welfare estimates.

scope_notes: All policies within a seed share Agents, activities, OD, weather, 12-vehicle pool, mode-choice shocks and base dispatch priorities.
A coupon binds to the first ride-hailing request; success redeems it and failure expires it for that modeled day-weather run.
C3 community/phone assistance enables one proxy booking only for an awarded elder-reserve coupon and does not change digital_access.
C4 runs three linked public-goods contribution rounds; every contribution changes the aggregate signal observed before other Agents' next-round decisions.
The public-goods multiplier changes virtual game payoffs only; the physical daily coupon pool remains exactly 10 or the number of participants when smaller.
